feat(multi-word): 統一多單字輸入處理，所有偵測到的單字都走完整流程

commit.message:
Fix IPv6 SMTP failure by resolving to IPv4 address explicitly

setDefaultResultOrder and family:4 are both ignored by Railway's
Node.js at the socket level. Using dns.resolve4() to fetch only A
records guarantees an IPv4 host is used, with tls.servername set
so certificate validation still works against smtp.gmail.com.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix code redemption: use POST to reliably link code to user account

The GET-based cookie auth wasn't reliably linking codes. Now the
redeem form uses a POST request that explicitly authenticates and
updates the access code email to the logged-in user.

commit.message:
feat: add Win32 API foreground check as alternative to a11y-based detection (#114)

* feat: add Win32 API foreground check as alternative to a11y-based detection

Add _check_foreground_win32() method that uses GetForegroundWindow() +
GetWindowText() via PowerShell P/Invoke for fast, reliable foreground
window title checking. This replaces the slow a11y-based check as the
default, while keeping a11y available via the focus_check_method config.

- New config field: focus_check_method (win32, a11y, or both)
- New CLI flag: --focus-check-method for run and live subcommands
- Detection of known-bad foreground states (Document Recovery, Start Center)
- Dispatch method routes to win32, a11y, or both (win32 first, a11y fallback)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update setup handler tests to mock win32 foreground check

The focus check default changed from a11y to win32, so tests need to
mock run_powershell instead of requests.get for the /accessibility
endpoint.

commit.message:
fix(crossplane): 3-stage bootstrap for Provider CRD ordering (#692) (#694)

* fix(crossplane): add 3rd stage for Provider CRs (#692)

Provider CRs (pkg.crossplane.io/v1) need:
  1. Crossplane core CRDs to exist (from infrastructure-crds)
  2. Time to download packages and register provider-specific CRDs

ProviderConfig CRs need those provider-specific CRDs to exist.

Putting Providers and ProviderConfigs in the same Flux Kustomization
fails because Flux applies atomically (dry-run rejects ProviderConfig
when CRDs don't exist yet). Putting Providers in infrastructure-crds
also fails because kstatus can't reliably detect when provider CRDs
are registered (Crossplane uses Installed/Healthy, not Ready).

Solution: 3-stage dependency chain:
  infrastructure-crds (Crossplane HelmRelease)
    → infrastructure-providers (Provider CRs, wait: true)
    → infrastructure (ProviderConfig + everything else)

The retryInterval on each stage self-heals any bootstrap race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct stage name in kustomization comment (#692)

The comment referenced infrastructure-crds but Provider packages
are in infrastructure-providers (stage 2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add infrastructure-providers to hardcoded kustomization lists (#692)

force-reconcile-all.py and bootstrap.py both have hardcoded
kustomization ordering/verification lists that need to include
the new infrastructure-providers stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update disaster-recovery dependency chain for 3-stage split (#692)

The dependency chain was missing infrastructure-providers, added by
the 3-stage Crossplane Provider CRD ordering fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(crossplane): use wait: false on infrastructure-providers (#692)

wait: true with kstatus is unreliable for Crossplane Provider CRs —
kstatus reports Current when Healthy=True is set, but CRD registration
happens asynchronously after that. A provider pod failure would also
block the entire chain for 15 minutes before timing out.

Stage 3's retryInterval: 1m is the correct mechanism for tolerating
the CRD registration race. This makes stage 2 consistent with stages
1 and 3 which both use wait: false for the same reason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[ACK-191] Add stale remote branch cleanup to critters clean (#164)

* [ACK-191] Add implementation plan

* [ACK-191] Add stale remote branch cleanup to critters clean

Add --branches flag to critters clean that finds and deletes stale
critter branches from remote repositories. A branch is stale if it has
a merged/closed PR, its issue is in a terminal state with no open PR,
or it has no PR and is older than 7 days. Branches with open PRs are
never deleted.

- Add deleteRemoteBranch() helper to git.ts
- Add loadBranchCleanConfig() for lightweight config loading without
  provider credential validation
- Add cleanStaleBranches() with PR, issue status, and age-based checks
- Update help text with --branches flag and clean-specific flags section

commit.message:
fix(licenses): extract items array from paginated API response

The list_licenses API returns {items:[], total, page, per_page, pages}
since Phase 6 pagination, but the JS assigned the whole dict to `licenses`
and called .filter() on it — which crashes since dicts don't have .filter().
This silently killed loadAll(), preventing map, stats, and table rendering.

Fix: extract licRes.items || [] before assignment.
Also add .catch() on boot calls to surface future errors in console.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix company research synthesis timeout: reduce Part 2 input tokens by 70%

Root cause: phaseSynthesizePart2 included full part1 report (~5000 tokens) +
full dossier (~5000 tokens) + prompt (~1250 tokens) = ~11250 input tokens.
At 60 tok/s under load, 5000 output tokens = 83s + latency > 90s budget.

Fixes:
- Extract only section headings from part1 (not full text): ~5000 tokens → ~50 tokens
- Truncate dossier for Part 2 to 8000 chars: ~5000 tokens → ~2000 tokens
- Reduce maxTokens for Part 2: 5000 → 4000 (4000 @ 60 tok/s = 67s safely in budget)
- Increase SYNTHESIS_PART_TIMEOUT_MS: 90s → 120s
- Update AbortController: 93s → 123s

Net result: Part 2 input ~3500 tokens (down from ~11000), fits comfortably in 120s budget.

fix(agentic-loop): populate tool result content from error for Gemini compat

When a tool execution fails, ToolResult has Error set but Content empty.
Gemini rejects tool messages with no content (400 INVALID_ARGUMENT).
Fall back to formatted error string when Content is empty.
